seo.title: Plausible Analytics vs Fathom vs Umami
seo.description: A practical comparison of Plausible Analytics, Fathom, and Umami for GDPR Compliance.

content.intro: Compare privacy-focused analytics tools for GDPR compliance, focusing on implementation effort, data control, and regulatory alignment.

options.name: Plausible Analytics
options.tagline: Simple, privacy-first analytics for small teams
options.best_for: Teams prioritizing minimal setup and compliance with explicit consent requirements

options.name: Fathom Analytics
options.best_for: Developers needing full control over data storage and processing

options.name: PostHog
options.tagline: Feature-rich, self-hosted analytics for growth teams
options.best_for: Organizations requiring advanced segmentation and event tracking

criteria.name: Implementation Effort
criteria.description: Complexity of integrating tracking code and configuring compliance settings

criteria.name: Lock-in Risk
criteria.description: Difficulty of migrating data or switching platforms

criteria.name: Data Control
criteria.description: Ability to host, delete, or audit user data

criteria.name: Compliance Features
criteria.description: Built-in tools for consent management and data deletion

values.PostHog: Free tier available (paid plans for enterprise)

verdict.summary: Plausible offers the simplest setup but lacks advanced compliance features. Fathom provides maximum control for developers comfortable with self-hosting. PostHog balances flexibility with enterprise-grade tools but requires more configuration.

use_cases.scenario: Startup needing quick deployment with minimal compliance overhead
use_cases.reason: Low implementation effort and transparent pricing align with rapid development cycles

use_cases.scenario: Developer team requiring full data sovereignty
use_cases.reason: Open-source architecture enables complete control over data handling and storage

use_cases.scenario: Growth-focused SaaS with complex tracking needs
use_cases.reason: Advanced segmentation and data deletion capabilities support evolving compliance requirements
